summary:
Root cause:
CsvReader was hard-coded to read the CSV file using the single-byte encoding ISO-8859-1.  
All names that contain characters not representable in that legacy code page (é, ü, ø, æ, etc.) were corrupted as soon as the file was read, so the generated report failed the tests.

Fix applied (in app/CsvReader.java):
1. Replaced the import and field initialisation:
   • removed Charset.forName("ISO-8859-1")  
   • now sets charset = StandardCharsets.UTF_8
2. Added an explanatory comment.

This makes CsvReader interpret the source file as UTF-8 – the encoding in which customers.csv is actually stored – so every international character is preserved end-to-end.

The updated code compiles and test.sh now passes, confirming that the “needle” (encoding bug) is fixed.
